Question Text: Darwin and Wallace’s theory of evolution by natural selection was revolutionary because it _______.
Option 1: was the first theory to refute the ideas of special creation
Option 2: proved that individuals acclimated to their environment over time
Option 3: dismissed the idea that species are constant and emphasized the importance of variation and change in populations
Option 4: was the first time a biologist had proposed that species changed through time

Question Text: The Greek philosopher Plato claimed that every organism was an example of a perfect essence created by God and that they were unchanging. What type of thinking is Plato’s description of species?
Option 1: Typological
Option 2: Organismal
Option 3: Evolutionary
Option 4: Scientifically supported

Question Text: Lamarck developed a hypothesis to explain the inheritance of traits that is often referred to as the “inheritance of acquired characters.” This idea suggests that _______.
Option 1: as an individual develops its genotype changes in response to challenges posed by the environment and it passes on phenotypic changes to offspring
Option 2: as an embryo develops its mother’s phenotype changes in response to challenges posed by the environment and the mother passes on these phenotypic changes to offspring
Option 3: as an individual develops its genotype changes in response to challenges posed by the environment and it passes on these genotypic changes to offspring
Option 4: as an individual develops its phenotype changes in response to challenges posed by the environment and it passes on these phenotypic changes to offspring

Question Text: When comparing Plato’s typological thinking with Darwin and Wallace’s theory of natural selection what is a key difference between these two approaches?
Option 1: Plato’s approach emphasizes gradual change while Darwin and Wallace propose abrupt changes.
Option 2: Plato’s approach is based on empirical observations while Darwin and Wallace’s theory relies on philosophical reasoning.
Option 3: Plato’s approach involves the idea of an ideal form while Darwin and Wallace’s theory emphasizes change and adaptation.
Option 4: Plato’s approach focuses on artificial selection while Darwin and Wallace’s theory centers on natural selection.

Question Text: Imagine two fossils are found in different layers of sedimentary rock. Fossil A is found in a lower layer and Fossil B is found in a higher layer. What can be inferred about the relative ages of these fossils?
Option 1: Fossil A is older than Fossil B.
Option 2: Fossil B is older than Fossil A.
Option 3: Fossil A and Fossil B are of the same age.
Option 4: You cannot tell without doing radiometric dating the relative ages of Fossil A and Fossil B.

Question Text: If x indicates the location of fossils of two closely related species then fossils of their most recent common ancestor are most likely to occur in which stratum? (A-top B-middle C-lower middle D-bottom)
Option 1: A
Option 2: B
Option 3: C
Option 4: D

Question Text: The giant sloth described by Georges Cuvier suggests that _______.
Option 1: organisms could not have originated by special creation
Option 2: organisms could go extinct
Option 3: the great flood or some other catastrophe caused the evolution of new species
Option 4: the Earth is old enough for evolution to have occurred

Question Text: Fossils of Thrinaxodon a species that lived during the Triassic period have been found in both South Africa and Antarctica. Thrinaxodon had a reptile-like skeleton and laid eggs but small depressions on the front of its skull suggest it had whiskers and therefore fur. Thrinaxodon may have been warm-blooded. The fossils of Thrinaxodon are consistent with the hypothesis that _______.
Option 1: fossils found in a given area look like the modern species in that same area
Option 2: the environment where it lived was very warm
Option 3: mammals evolved from a reptilian ancestor
Option 4: Antarctica and South Africa separated after Thrinaxodon went extinct

Question Text: What must be TRUE of any organ described as vestigial?
Option 1: It must be analogous to some feature in an ancestor.
Option 2: It must be homologous to some feature in an ancestor.
Option 3: It must be both homologous and analogous to some feature in an ancestor.
Option 4: It needs to be neither homologous nor analogous to some feature in an ancestor.

Question Text: Which of the pairs of plant structures below are homologous?
Option 1: Oak tree root and leaf
Option 2: Rose bush stem and flower
Option 3: Rose bush leaf and oak tree leaf
Option 4: There are no homologous plant structures.

Question Text: About thirteen different species of finches inhabit the Galápagos Islands today all descendants of a common ancestor from the South American mainland... If the six ground finch species have evolved most recently then which of these is the most logical prediction?
Option 1: They should be limited to the six islands that most recently emerged from the sea.
Option 2: They should be genetically more similar to each other than are the genomes of the five tree finch species.
Option 3: They should share fewer anatomical homologies with each other than they share with the tree finches.
Option 4: The chances of hybridization between two ground finch species should be less than the chances of hybridization between two tree finch species.

Question Text: Many crustaceans (e.g. lobsters shrimp and crayfish) use their tails to swim but crabs have reduced tails that curl under their shells and are not used in swimming. This is an example of _______.
Option 1: convergent evolution
Option 2: a homologous structure
Option 3: natural selection
Option 4: a vestigial trait

Question Text: Similar gill pouches in embryos of a chick human and cat are an example of _______.
Option 1: structural homology
Option 2: developmental homology
Option 3: genetic homology
Option 4: the inheritance of acquired characters

Question Text: The same basic internal organs (kidneys stomach heart and lungs) are found in the tetrapods including: lizards birds newts and mice. This is primarily an example of _______.
Option 1: structural homology
Option 2: developmental homology
Option 3: genetic correlation
Option 4: inheritance of acquired characteristics

Question Text: In the context of evolution what does the concept of “internal consistency” refer to?
Option 1: The uniformity of species across different environments
Option 2: The agreement between different lines of evidence supporting evolution
Option 3: The competition among individuals within a species
Option 4: The genetic diversity within a population

Question Text: Which piece of evidence supports the hypothesis that cetaceans (whales) evolved from terrestrial mammals as opposed to some aquatic organisms?
Option 1: A phylogeny of fossil cetaceans indicates that a gradual transition occurred between terrestrial forms and aquatic whale-like forms.
Option 2: DNA comparisons show that whales and hippos share more genetic sequences than whales share with other organisms.
Option 3: Some modern cetaceans have a vestigial hind limb buds.
Option 4: Whales arose from a terrestrial ancestor about 50 million years ago.
Option 5: Many independent lines of evidence converge on the conclusion that whales gradually evolved from a terrestrial mammalian ancestor about 50 million years ago.

Question Text: A dairy farmer chooses to mate a male bull only with the female heifers that always make the greatest amount of milk rather than heifers that produce a small amount of milk. What process is the dairy farmer employing when producing the next generation of calves?
Option 1: Natural selection
Option 2: Artificial selection
Option 3: Sexual selection
Option 4: Balancing selection

Question Text: Given a population that contains genetic variation what is the correct sequence of the following events under the influence of natural selection? (1. Well-adapted individuals leave more offspring. 2. A change occurs in the environment. 3. Genetic frequencies within the population change. 4. Poorly-adapted individuals have decreased survivorship.)
Biological fitness is best defined as _______.
Option 1: a heritable trait that increases the match of an individual to its environment
Option 2: variability in the population for a trait that increases survival
Option 3: the ability of an individual to produce offspring that survive and reproduce relative to other individuals in the population
Option 4: competition among individuals for resources that allow them to have more offspring
Option 5: similar reproductive success among individuals in a population

Question Text: If you say that a ground squirrel has greater evolutionary fitness than another ground squirrel in the same population you mean that the animal _______.
Option 1: has a longer life
Option 2: is able to mate more frequently
Option 3: is able to dominate other squirrels to gain access to food
Option 4: has more offspring that survive and reproduce themselves

Question Text: A species of birds living on an island has developed beaks of varying shapes and sizes due to differences in the types of available food. What term best describes the process by which these beak variations have evolved in response to food availability?
Option 1: Adaptation
Option 2: Selection
Option 3: Fitness
Option 4: Mutation

Question Text: A farmer uses triazine herbicide to control pigweed in his field... after several years the farmer sees more and more pigweed. Which of these explanations best explains what happened?
Option 1: The herbicide company lost its triazine formula and started selling poor-quality triazine.
Option 2: Natural selection caused the pigweed to mutate creating a new triazine-resistant species.
Option 3: Triazine-resistant pigweed has less efficient photosynthesis metabolism.
Option 4: Triazine-resistant weeds were more likely to survive and reproduce.

Question Text: Researchers discovered that a new strain of bacteria that causes tuberculosis (M. tuberculosis) has a point mutation in the rpoB gene... A researcher mixes M. tuberculosis with and without the rpoB mutation... and adds the bacteria to cell cultures [containing rifampin]. After many cell generations the researcher finds that _______.
Option 1: very few M. tuberculosis in the standard nutrient cell cultures carry the rpoB gene mutation but almost all of the M. tuberculosis in the cell cultures with rifampin carry the rpoB mutation
Option 2: almost all M. tuberculosis in the standard nutrient cell cultures carry the rpoB gene mutation but very few of the M. tuberculosis in the cell cultures with rifampin carry the rpoB mutation
Option 3: very few M. tuberculosis in any of the cell cultures carry the rpoB gene mutation
Option 4: almost all of the M. tuberculosis in both types of cell cultures carry the rpoB gene mutation
Option 5: a mix of both M. tuberculosis strains thrive in the standard cell cultures but no living bacteria can be found in the cell cultures that contain rifampin

Question Text: After the drought of 1977 researchers on the island of Daphne Major hypothesized that medium ground finches that had large deep beaks survived better... If this hypothesis is correct what would you expect to observe if a population of these medium ground finches colonizes a nearby island where T. cistoides is the most abundant food for the next 1000 years?
Option 1: Evolution of yet larger deeper beaks over time
Option 2: Evolution of smaller pointier beaks over time
Option 3: Random fluctuations in beak size and shape
Option 4: No change in beak size and shape

Question Text: After the drought of 1977 researchers hypothesized that on the Galápagos island Daphne Major medium ground finches with large deep beaks survived better... A tourist company sets up reliable feeding stations with a variety of bird seeds (different types and sizes)... Which of these events is now most likely to occur to finch beaks on this island?
Option 1: Evolution of yet larger deeper beaks over time until all birds have relatively large deep beaks
Option 2: Evolution of smaller pointier beaks over time until all birds have relatively small pointy beaks
Option 3: Increased variation in beak size and shape over time
Option 4: No change in beak size and shape over time

Question Text: Claytonia virginica is a woodland spring herb with flowers that vary from white to pale pink to bright pink... Slugs prefer to eat pink-flowering plants... Bees... prefer pink to white flowers... If the researcher removes all slugs from the study population what do you expect to happen to the distribution of flower colors in the population over time?
Option 1: The percentage of pink flowers should increase over time.
Option 2: The percentage of white flowers should increase over time.
Option 3: The distribution of flower colors should not change.
Option 4: The distribution of flower colors should randomly fluctuate over time.

Question Text: The accompanying figure shows the distribution of pocket-mouse coat colors in several Arizona populations... Mice with DD and Dd genotypes have dark coats whereas mice with the dd genotype are light colored. What sort of genotype frequencies might you expect to find in the Xmas Mid and O’Neill populations?
Option 1: Xmas-high DD frequency; Mid-high Dd frequency; O’Neill-high dd frequency
Option 2: Xmas-high Dd frequency; Mid-high DD frequency; O’Neill-high dd frequency
Option 3: Xmas-high Dd frequency; Mid-high dd frequency; O’Neill-high DD frequency
Option 4: Xmas-high dd frequency; Mid-high Dd frequency; O’Neill-high DD frequency
Option 5: Xmas-high dd frequency; Mid-high DD frequency; O’Neill-high Dd frequency

Question Text: DDT was once considered a “silver bullet” that would permanently eradicate insect pests... Which of these would have prevented this evolution of DDT resistance in insect pests?
Option 1: Larger doses of DDT should have been applied from the air into water and within the soil.
Option 2: All habitats should have received applications of DDT at about the same time.
Option 3: The frequency of DDT application should have been higher.
Option 4: None of the insect pests would have genetic variations that resulted in DDT resistance.

Question Text: If the bacterium Staphylococcus aureus experiences a cost for maintaining one or more antibiotic-resistance genes what would happen in environments that lack antibiotics?
Option 1: These genes would be maintained in case the antibiotics appear.
Option 2: These bacteria would be outcompeted and replaced by bacteria that have lost these genes.
Option 3: These bacteria would try to make the cost worthwhile by locating and migrating to microenvironments where traces of antibiotics are present.
Option 4: The number of genes conveying antibiotic resistance would increase in these bacteria.

Question Text: Which statement about the beak size of finches on the island of Daphne Major during prolonged drought is TRUE?
Option 1: Each bird evolved a deeper stronger beak as the drought persisted.
Option 2: Each bird’s survival was strongly influenced by the depth and strength of its beak as the drought persisted.
Option 3: Each bird that survived the drought produced only offspring with deeper stronger beaks than seen in the previous generation.
Option 4: The frequency of the strong-beak alleles increased in each bird as the drought persisted.

Question Text: “Explain why it is inaccurate to say that natural selection changes individuals.” Which of the following statements provides the most accurate explanation for this inaccuracy?
Option 1: Natural selection primarily acts on populations shaping the traits of populations of species rather than individual organisms.
Option 2: Individual organisms change due to environmental pressures but these changes are not considered natural selection.
Option 3: Natural selection works by actively altering the genetic makeup of individual organisms to fit their environment.
Option 4: Natural selection can only change the physical appearance of individuals but not their genetic composition.

Question Text: Which of the following statements accurately represents the concept of evolution?
Option 1: Evolution is a purposeful process aimed at creating perfect organisms.
Option 2: Evolution has a specific goal of producing complex life forms.
Option 3: Evolution is a non-goal-directed process.
Option 4: Evolution is driven by external forces guiding species toward specific outcomes.

Question Text: Vestigial traits and neutral changes in DNA sequences are good examples of _______.
Option 1: adaptation
Option 2: convergent traits
Option 3: nonadaptive traits
Option 4: developmental homology

Question Text: Which of the following is a fitness trade-off (compromise)?
Option 1: In some hornbill species the male helps seal the female in a tree with her nest until the young are ready to fledge.
Option 2: Hummingbirds are the best pollinators of certain flowers but bees are the best pollinators for orchids.
Option 3: The strong thick beak of a woodpecker helps it find insects in trees.
Option 4: Turtle shells provide protection but are heavy and burdensome when moving.

Question Text: Over long periods of time many cave-dwelling organisms have lost their eyes... How can natural selection account for these losses?
Option 1: Natural selection cannot account for losses but accounts only for new structures and functions.
Option 2: Natural selection accounts for these losses by the principle of use and disuse.
Option 3: Under particular circumstances that persisted for long periods each of these structures presented greater costs than benefits.
Option 4: The ancestors of these organisms experienced harmful mutations that forced them to lose these structures.

Question Text: A proficient engineer can easily design skeletal structures that are more functional than those currently found in the forelimbs of such diverse mammals as horses whales and bats... The actual forelimbs of these mammals do NOT seem to be optimally arranged because _______.
Option 1: natural selection has not had sufficient time to create the optimal design in each case but will do so given enough time
Option 2: in many cases phenotype is determined by genotype and the environment
Option 3: though we may not consider the fit between the current skeletal arrangements and their functions excellent we should not doubt that natural selection ultimately produces the best design
Option 4: natural selection is generally limited to modifying structures that were present in previous generations and/or in previous species

Question Text: Evolutionary Theory Predicts That Species Are Related Not Independent. Four Of The Following Examples Provide Support For This Prediction But One Is Irrelevant. Which One Of These Examples Does Not Support The Claim That Species Are Related?

Option 1: Condition 1 Only

Question Text: A Patient Has Finished His Treatment For A Bacterial Infection With An Antibiotic And Feels Better. However Four Days Later The Same Bacterial Infection Returns. The Doctor Prescribes The Same Course Of Antibiotics But This Time These Antibiotics Do Not Cure The Patient. What Would Be The Most Likely Reason For This?
Option 1: The Antibiotic Was Quickly Metabolized By The Patient And Did Not Have Time To Work.
Option 2: The Non-Resistant Bacteria Evolved Within These Four Days So That The Drug Became Ineffective.
Option 3: The Patient Was Not Treated In Time And So The Infection Became Too Severe.
Option 4: The Drug Killed Most Of The Bacteria In The Population But A Few Survived Due To The Presence Of A Resistance Gene And Were Subsequently Able To Reproduce And Pass This Gene Onto Their Offspring.

Question Text: If The Population [of mixed Bacteria] Is Maintained On Media That Contains Rifampin What Is Expected To Occur Over A Period Of Time?

Option 3: The Rifampin Susceptible Bacteria And The Rifampin Resistant Bacteria Will Remain In Approximately Equal Proportions.

Question Text: If The Population [of mixed Bacteria] Is Maintained On Media That Lacks Rifampin What Is Expected To Occur Over A Period Of Time?
Option 1: The Rifampin Resistant Bacteria Will Increase In Proportion Compared To The Rifampin Susceptible Bacteria.

Option 3: The Rifampin Susceptible Bacteria And The Rifampin Resistant Bacteria Will Remain In Approximately Equal Proportions.

Question Text: The Oppresso Bird Preys On These Beetles. What Do You Predict Will Be The Outcome Of This Predation Over Time?

Question Text: Consider Gene A Which Has Two (2) Alleles In The Population. A4 Codes For A Mutated Allele... A2 Is The Normal Allele... Based On This Information Can Natural Selection Eliminate The A4 Allele?

Question Text: A Population Of Rabbits Grows Explosively Because Resources Are High. However Now That Numbers Have Increased Substantially There Is Not Enough Food For All Individuals And The Population Is Threatened. What Will Happen Under Such Circumstances?
Option 1: The Rabbits Will Share The Food With Each Other; If Everyone Gets A Little Bit Of Food The Population Will Survive.
Option 2: A Random Group Of The Rabbits Will Survive And Reproduce To Form The Next Generation.
Option 3: There Will Be Competition For The Food And Some Rabbits Will Not Survive To Reproduce.
Option 4: Some Rabbits Will Move Away To Find Another Food Source So That Individuals Remaining In The Population Will Survive.

Question Text: According To The Theory Of Evolution By Natural Selection Which Of The Following Is FALSE?
Option 1: The Individuals That Survive To Reproduce Are A Random Sample Of The Population.
Option 2: Natural Selection Occurs At The Level Of The Individual But The Changes (Evolution) Are Observed At The Population Level.
Option 3: Over-Reproduction And Limited Resources Leads To Competition.
Option 4: Individuals With Certain Heritable Traits Contribute More Reproductively To The Next Generation Than Others Without Those Traits.

Question Text: Fishers Typically Target The Largest And Thus Oldest Fish. Which Of The Following Best Predicts The Effect Of Natural Selection Due To This Fishing Strategy On The Life History Traits Of The Commercially Popular Fish?
Option 1: A Greater Proportion Of The Fish Population Would Be Made Up Of Individuals That Become Reproductively Mature At A Smaller Size And Thus An Earlier Age.
Option 2: A Greater Proportion Of The Fish Population Would Be Made Up Of Individuals That Become Reproductively Mature At A Larger Size And Thus An Older Age.
Option 3: There Would Be No Change In The Life Histories Of These Fish Populations As A Species' Age At Reproduction Cannot Change.
Option 4: A Greater Proportion Of The Fish Population Would Be Made Up Of Individuals That Become Reproductively Mature At A Smaller Size And Thus An Older Age.

Question Text: The Changes In The Commercially Popular Fish Populations' Life History Traits Is An Example Of What Type Of Selection Pressure?
Option 1: Strong
